requirement.original: OOS item B — Write-guard sentinel integration. The hooks/pretool-write-guard.sh blocks all Write-tool calls that would overwrite an existing file, with no bypass mechanism. During prior dev cycles, this caused legitimate orchestrated overwrites of stale QA reports to fail, requiring hacky workarounds (_recheck.json fallback paths, manual cp -f). The sentinel-grant system in hooks/lib/allowlist.py already handles Bash-command grants, but has no matcher for the Write tool's overwrite operation. The fix is to integrate: (1) a Write-overwrite matcher in allowlist.py (matching on session_id + operation='Write' + target-path), (2) a sentinel check in pretool-write-guard.sh before the existing-file block, (3) consumption of Write grants in posttool-allowlist-consume.py on any terminal Write result.
requirement.clarified: Add match_sentinel_grant_for_write(task_id, session_id, target_path) to allowlist.py; add sentinel check (with CF-1 invariant parity) to pretool-write-guard.sh before the existing-file block; extend posttool-allowlist-consume.py sentinel consumption to Write tool events. No changes to grant creation (/allow) or grant schema.
requirement.what: Three-file sentinel-grant integration for Write-overwrite authorization
requirement.why: Legitimate orchestrated Write-overwrites of stale artifacts are blocked by pretool-write-guard.sh with no bypass mechanism, forcing hacky workarounds across multiple dev cycles

included: pretool-write-guard.sh: hoist file_path/task_id parsing; add sentinel check with CF-1 suppression of legacy grant when sentinel exists-but-no-match
posttool-allowlist-consume.py: add Write tool sentinel consumption branch

success_criteria: match_sentinel_grant_for_write() exists in allowlist.py; returns matched entry on session+target match, None on mismatch/expired
pretool-write-guard.sh exits 0 when sentinel matches; suppresses legacy grant when sentinel exists-but-no-match
posttool-allowlist-consume.py consumes Write sentinel on terminal Write result
git diff HEAD shows only 3 files modified (+ optional test file)
constraints: allowed_operations[] field key for path match MUST be 'target' (not 'target_path') per existing schema in allowlist.py:22
session_id MUST be validated in match_sentinel_grant_for_write (not just task_id)
CF-1 invariant: when sentinel exists for task but does NOT match write target, legacy read_grant('Write') MUST be suppressed
FILE_PATH and task-id MUST be parsed before the sentinel check in write-guard.sh
posttool Write branch MUST use tool_input.file_path, NOT the Bash 'command' variable
Subagent exemption preserved: sentinel check only inside the _WG_IS_SUB != '1' guard

out_of_path_observed_but_not_touched: hooks/userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh (grant creation — read for context only)

root_cause_analysis.symptom: Write tool calls that target existing files are blocked by pretool-write-guard.sh even when the orchestrator has legitimate authorization via sentinel grant
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: pretool-write-guard.sh has no sentinel-grant check path; allowlist.py has no match_sentinel_grant_for_write() function; posttool-allowlist-consume.py restricts sentinel consumption to tool_name == 'Bash' only (line 105)
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A — architectural gap, not a regression
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: Sentinel-grant system (task 20260519-211515 R2) was designed for Bash commands; Write-tool sentinel support was explicitly deferred as out-of-scope (CF2-10 OBSERVATION_ONLY in prior cycle context.json)
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: Every write to an existing artifact by a non-subagent main-agent is blocked with no bypass, forcing fallback paths (_recheck.json suffixes, cp -f workarounds) that pollute artifact namespacing and break canonical report paths
root_cause_analysis.timeline: First documented: task 20260519-211515 (CF2-10 OBSERVATION_ONLY). Recurred in: dev-report-d1e94e.json, dev-report-20260519-211515-iter3.json, qa-output-ba-validation-20260519-211515.json, completion-20260519-211515-redev9items.md

context.codebase_state: master branch, clean working tree for target files
context.recent_commits: 1a1d058 feat(dev-cycle): Cycle 2 remediation; 50f2ca2 fix(agents): tighten style-inspector; 1bf636c chore(docs): ship cycle 20260519-211515 close-report

development_approach.strategy: Three surgical additions mirroring existing sentinel-grant patterns: (1) add match_sentinel_grant_for_write() to allowlist.py following match_sentinel_grant_for_bash_command() shape; (2) hoist FILE_PATH+task_id parsing in write-guard.sh and add sentinel check with CF-1 suppression; (3) add Write branch to posttool sentinel block using file_path not command
development_approach.validation_approach: Unit-test match_sentinel_grant_for_write with synthesized sentinel files; smoke-test write-guard.sh with crafted stdin payloads targeting existing files with/without a sentinel grant

_risk_level_doc: medium — changes hook infrastructure (PreToolUse/PostToolUse) but only adds new bypass paths, never removes existing guards. Blast radius: 3 files. No auth boundary, no schema change, no user-triggered pipeline code.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex identified 5 real bugs in the initial draft: (1) session_id missing from function signature; (2) field name 'target_path' vs existing schema 'target'; (3) FILE_PATH parsing order in write-guard.sh (must precede sentinel check); (4) posttool must use file_path not command for Write; (5) CF-1 invariant parity — sentinel-exists-but-no-match must suppress legacy grant. All 5 incorporated.

findings.issue: match_sentinel_grant_for_write signature lacks session_id; no session validation in draft
findings.proposed_fix: Implement match_sentinel_grant_for_write(task_id, session_id, target_path); require grant['session_id'] == session_id before entry iteration
findings.rationale: Without session_id check, a grant for one session could be consumed by a different session's write-guard invocation; accepted and incorporated into M1 and AC1

findings.issue: Draft used 'target_path' field; existing schema uses 'target'
findings.proposed_fix: Match against entry.get('target') for exact path equality; do not introduce new field name 'target_path'
findings.rationale: Consistency with existing schema documented at allowlist.py:22 and used by match_sentinel_grant_for_bash_command; incorporated into M1, AC1, and Technical Hints (dev constraint)

findings.issue: pretool-write-guard.sh extracts FILE_PATH after the legacy /allow block (line 49-50); sentinel check placed 'after lines 31-46' would lack the file path; also task-id fallback chain incomplete
findings.proposed_fix: Parse FILE_PATH, session_id, and task_id BEFORE sentinel check; task_id fallback: input task_id field → CLAUDE_TASK_ID env → session_id
findings.rationale: Structural ordering issue; incorporated into M2, AC2, Risk 3, and Technical Hints

findings.issue: posttool Write branch must not reuse command variable (Bash-specific); needs tool_input.file_path
findings.proposed_fix: Add elif tool_name == 'Write': branch extracting file_path = (data.get('tool_input') or {}).get('file_path', ''); call match_sentinel_grant_for_write(task_id, session_id, file_path)
findings.rationale: Write tool has no 'command' field; using command would always be empty string, never matching; incorporated into M3, AC3, Risk 4

findings.issue: Draft places sentinel check after legacy /allow; if sentinel exists-but-no-match, legacy grant could still bypass (weakens target-path strict semantics)
findings.proposed_fix: Mirror Bash CF-1 invariant: when sentinel exists for task but does not match Write target, suppress legacy read_grant('Write') fallback
findings.rationale: Critical parity with existing Bash sentinel invariant documented in pretool-bash-safety.sh:516-526; incorporated into M2, AC2, Risk 1

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 5 codex findings were blockers with PROPOSED_FIX; all incorporated. Function signature expanded to include session_id (Finding 1). Field name corrected to 'target' (Finding 2). FILE_PATH/task_id hoisting documented as explicit requirement (Finding 3). posttool Write branch uses file_path not command (Finding 4). CF-1 invariant added to M2 and AC2 (Finding 5).
